# Paneflow

> A native terminal workspace for orchestrating Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, and other CLI coding agents. Split, supervise, and automate parallel agent sessions — Linux first, macOS available, Windows porting in progress.

Paneflow is open-source software released under the MIT license. The marketing site (https://paneflow.dev) is a small static surface — four pages today (home, download, about, privacy), expanding with comparison content over the next quarter. The application source lives at https://github.com/ArthurDEV44/paneflow.

This file is published strictly as crawl hygiene — it is NOT a ranking signal, retrieval boost, or AEO/GEO lever. It exists so cooperative LLM crawlers that respect llmstxt.org can identify the canonical content surface efficiently.

## Pages

- [Home](https://paneflow.dev/): Product overview for the terminal workspace and orchestration layer for CLI coding agents.
- [Download](https://paneflow.dev/download): Installation instructions and release artifacts.
- [About](https://paneflow.dev/about): Founder bio (Arthur Jean, Strivex) and project origins.

- [Compare: Paneflow vs WezTerm](https://paneflow.dev/compare/paneflow-vs-wezterm): Detailed comparison (forthcoming — US-018).
